short_claim_summary: Admissible parameter-space collapse is possible only through reduction of standing-bearing role-rank components across the carrier stack or through obstruction of would-be role directions.
verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Admissible parameter-space collapse is possible only through reduction of standing-bearing role-rank components across the carrier stack or through obstruction of would-be role directions. A raw coordinate reduction that does not change any role-rank or obstruction class is bookkeeping, not numerical collapse. 10 Proof. Parameter-space collapse is a statement about standing numerical possibilities. Standing numerical possibilities are counted or bounded by carrier role-ranks and obstruction classes. If a proposed reduction changes neither the role-rank vector nor the obstruction map, then it preserves the same standing-bearing possibilities and merely redescribes the coordinate chart. Genuine collapse must therefore reduce role-rank support or record a standing obstruction. 10 Global obstruction map

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Constraint 2.4 (Target-Class Entry Gate). Let a construction system purport to operate over a fixed domain with a determinate admissibility boundary, irreversible evaluation, identity preservation, stable reuse, and terminal breakdown handling. Then any construction sequence that denies admissibility, standing, fixed-domain constraint, identity preservation, irreversibility, or collapse semantics fails to instantiate the target class. Conversely, any system instantiating the target class must realize those constraints under some notation. Constraint demonstration. Irreversible evaluation requires a boundary between admitted and non-admitted construction sequences; without such a boundary, no evaluated state is determinately retained as an evaluated state. Identity preservation requires that construction states remain fixed across admissible continuation; without it, later use is not reuse of the same constructed state. Stable reuse requires a standing status; without standing, prior admissibility does not persist as a usable status. Fixed-domain operation requires a scope lock; without it, apparent continuation is silent domain replacement. Terminal breakdown handling requires collapse semantics; without it, failed construction sequences can be laundered by same-trajectory repair

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Theorem 4.4 (Quotient necessity for physical standing). If a physical domain supports reusable same-domain physical content under lawful redescription, then its standing-bearing content factors through a quotient by admissible redescription. Proof. Let p, p′ ∈ PX be two presentations related by lawful redescription and suppose they are not identified in standing content. Then the domain distinguishes them as different physical contents despite the absence of an admissible witness separating them. This either privileges one representation as identity-grounding or imports a hidden discriminator not present in the physical observable structure. Both violate the kernel: representation privilege violates reference stability, and hidden discrimination violates standing because it cannot be reused as licit same-domain observable content. Hence lawfully redescribed states must be identified. Conversely, a quotient by lawful redescription preserves exactly those distinctions that survive admissible witnesses and continuation. Thus standing-bearing content factors through PX / ∼X .

If the late scope does not contain the data and lawful redescription rules required to construct an exchangeability preservation certificate, then no exact matter-antimatter exchange map is recoverable as a same-scope identity symmetry. In particular, global microstate retention is irrelevant to the theorem unless it induces such a certificate from the data admitted by S. Proof. Recovering exact exchangeability as a same-scope identity symmetry requires more than an external statement that some total description contains correlations. It requires a lawful map, available from the late scope’s admitted data and redescription rules, that carries the pre-irreversible exchange involution into the post-irreversible anchor quotient while preserving carrier-role profiles, downstream material-reference roles, admissible continuation, and the sign-coherent burden of the scope. That map is exactly an exchangeability preservation certificate. If the late scope lacks the data or rules needed to construct it, then any alleged recovery uses hidden external indexing, a selector, or a changed theorem object rather than same-scope admissible recovery. Hence exact exchange

The dynamical uniqueness lemma proved earlier in this section then identifies the canonical local vacuum sector of T(G) e with the predecessor vacuum representation, and Theorem VIII.33 shows that every further same-theory enlargement falls under the completion classification theorem already proved. If, on the other hand, T(G) e is not a realization of the same theory on the same domain, then by Theorem VIII.77 the failure of local fixation is not a harmless same-domain difference: it changes the bounded local observable/dynamical content and therefore defines a different theory object.
